hero.title: THE INVISIBLE ARCHITECTURE OF THE QURAN
hero.subtitle: Linguistic, Mathematical & Scientific Evidence
hero.description: Beneath the surface of a 1,400-year-old text lies an order invisible to the naked eye. Every word measured, every sound calculated, every story part of a grand design. Modern science is only now beginning to decode it.

mathMiracle.title: The Impossible Balance of Numbers
mathMiracle.intro: The Quran was revealed over 23 years, piece by piece, in response to different events and circumstances. If a book's chapters were written at different times, in different contexts, could the word counts within it be perfectly balanced by coincidence? Statisticians estimate the probability at less than 1 in 10,000.

seaLand.title: Sea and Land Ratio
seaLand.ratioNote: This matches Earth's actual sea-to-land ratio exactly!

mathMiracle.closing: These numbers emerge from thousands of verses spread across different chapters, revealed over 23 years. No human could plan this - because no one could know which verse would be revealed when.

linguisticDNA.title: A 1,400-Year-Old Code
linguisticDNA.intro: Twenty-nine chapters of the Quran open with mysterious letters: Alif-Lam-Mim. Ha-Mim. Ya-Sin. No one knows for certain what they mean. A code that has remained unsolved for 1,400 years. But here is what we do know: these 14 letters make up exactly half of the 28-letter Arabic alphabet... and account for 70% of all letters used in the Quran. Why exactly these 14 letters were chosen, why precisely half the alphabet — questions that have gone unanswered for 1,400 years. A cipher? A system of balance? Only Allah knows.

letters.label: Unique Letters
letters.description: Half of the Arabic alphabet (14 out of 28)

suras.label: Chapters
suras.description: 29 out of 114 chapters begin with these letters

coverage.label: Letter Coverage
coverage.description: Approximately 70% of all Quranic letters consist of these 14 letters

kaf.title: The Mathematics of Sura Qaf
kaf.description: The letter Qaf (ق) appears exactly 57 times in Sura Qaf. 57 x 2 = 114 - the total number of chapters in the Quran.

checksum.title: The Digital Checksum Analogy ⓘ
checksum.description: Some researchers have proposed that, just as modern digital systems use checksums to protect data integrity, these letters may have served a similar built-in verification function for the Quranic text. This remains an academic hypothesis — the Quran itself does not explain the purpose of these letters.

impossibleRhythm.title: Neither Poetry Nor Prose
impossibleRhythm.intro: In 7th-century Arabia, language existed in two forms: rigid poetry bound to 16 strict meters, or free-form prose. No other category was conceivable. When the Quran arrived, the Arabs were stunned. This text fit none of the 16 meters - yet it was not prose either. It was rhythmic but unmeasured. Disciplined yet free. A form never heard before. Linguists call it sui generis - a category unto itself. The Quran's language created its own genre in the history of literature.
impossibleRhythm.poetryDesc: Rigid structure bound to 16 strict meters
impossibleRhythm.quranDesc: Rhythmic yet meterless - a form never seen before

examples.detail: 62 consecutive verses ending with the same sound
Just 3 verses, but a rhythm so powerful it silenced poets

impossibleRhythm.quote: A rhythm without meter, freedom within discipline.

soundArchitecture.title: These Sounds Are No Coincidence
soundArchitecture.intro: Read the verses about punishment and hellfire aloud. You will hear harsh, explosive consonants: ta, qaf, kaf, ta. Your tongue and palate collide. Your body tenses. Now read the verses about paradise and mercy. You will hear soft, flowing sounds: mim, nun, lam, ra. Your breathing eases. Your heart slows.

punishment.title: Verses of Punishment
punishment.description: Dominated by harsh, explosive consonants: ta, qaf, kaf, ta. Your tongue and palate collide, your body tenses.

mercy.title: Verses of Mercy
mercy.description: Dominated by soft, flowing sounds: mim, nun, lam, ra. Your breathing eases, your heart slows.

neuroscience.title: Neuroscientific Evidence
neuroscience.description: Modern neuroscience shows that harsh consonants activate the amygdala (the brain's fear center), while soft sounds trigger oxytocin release. fMRI studies have confirmed this.

soundArchitecture.closing: The Quran communicates through sound BEFORE meaning.

hiddenSymmetry.title: A Mirror Within a Mirror
hiddenSymmetry.intro: Consider Sura Al-Fatiha. Seven verses. Seems simple. But look at its structure: a perfect mirror symmetry. A-B-C-CENTER-C'-B'-A'. According to Raymond Farrin's research, 70% of the Quran's chapters carry this structure. Known as 'ring composition,' it took centuries to discover.

layers.label: A — Allah's Name
layers.text: Opens with the name of Allah

layers.label: C — The Most Merciful
layers.text: The Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate

layers.label: C' — Worship
layers.text: You alone we worship (response to Mercy)

layers.label: A' — Those Who Received Grace
layers.text: Those who received grace (response to Allah's name)

stat.label: Ring Composition Rate
stat.description: According to Raymond Farrin's research, 70% of the Quran's chapters exhibit this mirror symmetry structure. This structure was unknown in 7th-century Arabic literature.

hiddenSymmetry.closing: This structure was unknown in 7th-century Arabic literature. Ring composition was only identified through modern literary analysis.

sevenLayers.title: One Verse, Seven Universes
sevenLayers.intro: Verse 35 of Sura An-Nur is one of the most extensively interpreted verses in the Quran. Ibn Arabi, Ghazali, Razi - the great scholars wrote volumes on this single verse. Why? Because like light passing through a prism, this verse reveals a different reality from every angle.

verse.translation: Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth...

layers.title: Literal (Physical)
layers.description: Light, lamp, glass, olive oil - a physical description of illumination

layers.title: Metaphoric (Spiritual)
layers.description: Divine guidance as light piercing through darkness

layers.title: Cosmological (Scientific)
layers.description: Light as the fundamental force of the universe, photons, the electromagnetic spectrum

layers.title: Epistemological (Philosophical)
layers.description: Knowledge as illumination, ignorance as darkness

layers.title: Psychological (Inner World)
layers.description: The inner light of the soul, consciousness, awareness

layers.title: Mystical (Sufi)
layers.description: The Sufi concept of divine light (Nur al-Muhammadi), stages of enlightenment

layers.title: Theological (Divine)
layers.description: Allah as the source of all existence, light beyond light

sevenLayers.closing: These seven layers demonstrate how deeply multi-dimensional a single verse can be. Each perspective illuminates a different reality.

scientificSigns.title: What Science Discovered 1,400 Years Later
scientificSigns.intro: The Quran is not a book of science. But certain verses point to realities that modern science would not discover for centuries. Here are four striking examples.

iron.title: The Cosmic Journey of Iron
iron.content: The Quran uses a peculiar word for iron: 'sent down' (anzalna). Not 'created' - sent down. In the 7th century, this would have seemed illogical. Iron comes from the ground, found in mines. Why would it be 'sent down'? Modern astrophysics provides the answer: Iron CANNOT form in stars like our Sun. The temperature is insufficient. Iron can only be produced in the supernova explosions of massive stars, then scattered into space. All the iron on Earth arrived from space - via meteorites - billions of years ago. Iron was literally 'sent down.' And one more detail: Sura Al-Hadid (Iron) is the 57th chapter of the Quran. The most common isotope of iron? Fe-57.

verse.arabic: وَأَنزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ
verse.translation: We sent down iron, wherein is great might and benefits for humanity.

facts: Hadid 57:25 - The word 'anzalna' (we sent down) is used specifically for iron
Iron cannot form in stars like our Sun (insufficient temperature)
Iron is only produced in supernovae and scattered into space
All iron on Earth arrived via meteorites billions of years ago
Sura Al-Hadid = 57th chapter, Fe-57 = most common iron isotope

universe.title: The Expanding Universe
universe.content: In 1929, Edwin Hubble discovered that galaxies are moving away from us. The universe was expanding. This was one of the greatest discoveries in the history of science. The Quran, 1,400 years earlier, states: 'We constructed the heaven with power, and indeed, We are its expander.' The word 'Musi'un' uses the present continuous tense, indicating ongoing expansion. Not a description of a static universe - but an active, continuing expansion. Precisely what Hubble discovered.

verse.translation: We constructed the heaven with power, and indeed, We are its expander.

facts: 1929: Edwin Hubble discovered the expansion of the universe
The word 'Musi'un' indicates continuous expansion (present tense)
In Sura Al-Anbiya 21:30, 'ratq' (joined) and 'fatq' (split apart) - pointing to the Big Bang

ocean.title: The Ocean Barrier
ocean.content: Two seas flow side by side, yet they do not mix. An invisible barrier separates them. This is what modern oceanography calls a 'halocline' - a boundary layer caused by differences in salinity. It can be observed at the Strait of Gibraltar, the Bosphorus, and the Red Sea. Jacques Cousteau documented this phenomenon in his films in the 1960s. The Quran describes this with the word 'barzakh' (barrier). Two bodies of water meet but do not 'transgress' upon each other - meaning no sudden mixing occurs. This is the precise definition of a halocline.

verse.arabic: مَرَجَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ يَلْتَقِيَانِ ﴿١٩﴾ بَيْنَهُمَا بَرْزَخٌ لَّا يَبْغِيَانِ
verse.translation: He released the two seas, meeting side by side. Between them is a barrier which they do not transgress.

facts: Halocline: a natural boundary layer caused by differences in salinity
Observed at the Strait of Gibraltar, the Bosphorus, and the Red Sea
Jacques Cousteau documented this phenomenon in the 1960s

embryo.title: Embryology - The Word 'Alaqah'
embryo.content: The Quran describes the second stage of the human embryo with the word 'alaqah.' This word carries THREE meanings in Arabic - and all three are facts confirmed by modern embryology. In the 7th century, there were no microscopes. The implantation process was invisible to the eye. The embryo measured just 1-2 mm. Where did this knowledge come from?

meanings.explanation: The embryo CLINGS to the uterine wall (implantation, days 6-12)
The early embryo looks like a RED MASS as blood vessels form
The embryo feeds on the mother's blood and resembles a LEECH in shape (C-shaped, rounded head)

facts: Dr. Keith L. Moore: "The correlation between Quranic descriptions of the embryo and modern science is remarkable."
The Quran states bones form BEFORE muscles (Mu'minun 23:14) - modern embryology confirms this
Galen claimed the opposite (muscles first) - the Quran was right

historicalProof.title: Three Stories Confirmed by History
historicalProof.intro: For centuries, details in the Quran were dismissed as 'errors.' Modern archaeology and linguistics proved them right. Here are three striking examples.

pharaoh.title: The Pharaoh's Body
pharaoh.subtitle: ~1200 BCE — Confirmed in 1881
pharaoh.content: Sura Yunus 10:92 - God tells the drowning Pharaoh: 'Today We will preserve your body so that you may be a sign for those who come after you.' A strange promise. The Torah makes no mention of the Pharaoh's body being preserved. It simply says he 'drowned' and closes the story.
points: The Torah is silent about the Pharaoh's body (Exodus 14:28 - just 'drowned')
1881: 3,000-year-old mummies discovered at Deir el-Bahari
Ramesses II's mummy is now displayed at the Cairo Egyptian Museum
Salt crystals found in the body (possible seawater evidence)
In the 7th century, the existence of these mummies was unknown

verse.arabic: فَالْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ آيَةً
verse.translation: Today We will preserve your body so that you may be a sign for those who come after you.

haman.title: The Haman Code
haman.subtitle: ~1300 BCE — Confirmed in 1935
haman.content: For centuries, critics claimed the Quran contained an 'error': the name Haman. In the Torah, Haman lives in the Persian Empire (~480 BCE). In the Quran, Haman appears alongside the Pharaoh (~1300 BCE). An 800-year discrepancy!
points: 800-year discrepancy between the Torah's Haman and the Quran's Haman
Rosetta Stone (1799) → Champollion (1822) → Hieroglyphs decoded
Ranke's encyclopedia (1935): 'Hm-n-h' found in Egyptian records
Role: overseer of stone workers, construction manager - New Kingdom period
The Quran describes Haman as a construction official - historically verified

rome.title: Rome's Impossible Victory
rome.subtitle: 614-628 CE — Confirmed by historical records
rome.content: 614 CE: The Sasanian Persians capture Jerusalem. The Byzantine Empire is on the verge of collapse. No one believes Byzantium can recover. At precisely this time, the Quran offers a startling prophecy.
points: 'Bid'i sineen' (a few years) = 3-9 years in Arabic
From 622 to 628 = 6 years - right within the boundaries
'Adna al-ard' means both 'the nearest land' and 'the lowest land'
The Dead Sea area: 430 meters below sea level - the lowest point on Earth
This geographical fact was only discovered in the 19th century

verse.arabic: الم ﴿١﴾ غُلِبَتِ الرُّومُ ﴿٢﴾ فِي أَدْنَى الْأَرْضِ وَهُم مِّن بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ ﴿٣﴾ فِي بِضْعِ سِنِينَ
verse.translation: Alif-Lam-Mim. The Romans have been defeated in the nearest land. But after their defeat, they will triumph within a few years.

livingPreservation.title: The Only Unchanged Text on Earth
livingPreservation.intro: The Quran in Mecca = the Quran in Medina = Istanbul = Cairo = Jakarta. Letter for letter, identical. 1.8 billion Muslims, different denominations, different countries, different languages. Yet they all read the SAME text. Variation: zero.

huffaz.label: Huffaz (Worldwide)
huffaz.description: People who have memorized the entire Quran

variation.label: Textual Variation
variation.description: Zero differences across all copies worldwide

written.title: Written Preservation
written.description: Over 40 scribes during the Prophet's lifetime, the first compilation under Abu Bakr, standardization and distribution to 7 cities under Uthman.

oral.title: Living Preservation
oral.description: Over 10 million huffaz (people who have memorized the entire Quran) worldwide. Each hafiz learned from their teacher, who learned from theirs - an unbroken chain reaching back to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

livingPreservation.experiment: A thought experiment: if every written copy of the Quran vanished overnight, huffaz could reconstruct it letter by letter. This is true of no other book in existence.

birmingham.title: Birmingham Manuscript
birmingham.description: 2015: A manuscript at the University of Birmingham was carbon-dated to 568-645 CE - overlapping with the Prophet Muhammad's ﷺ own lifetime!

zeroRedundancy.title: Every Word Carries a Purpose
zeroRedundancy.intro: At first glance, the Quran appears to repeat itself. The story of Moses is told in over 30 chapters. But each retelling emphasizes a DIFFERENT aspect. This is not redundancy - it is pedagogical design. Like a textbook: teaching the same subject from different angles.
zeroRedundancy.mosesTitle: The Story of Moses - Different Chapters, Different Lessons

mosesExamples.theme: The rebellion of the Israelites (theme of ingratitude)
Confrontation with the sorcerers (truth versus falsehood)

totalWords.label: Total Words
totalWords.description: Total word count in the Quran

zeroRedundancy.zemahseriQuote: Every word of the Quran is a treasure. Remove one, and the entire edifice collapses.

cards.title: The Prefrontal Cortex
cards.content: The Quran says the liar will be seized by his 'forelock' - the forehead (Al-Alaq 96:15-16). Modern neuroscience: the prefrontal cortex (directly behind the forehead) is the center of lying, moral reasoning, and deception. Brain functions were unknown in the 7th century.

cards.title: Fingerprints
cards.content: 'We are able to restore even his fingertips' (Al-Qiyama 75:4). Why mention fingertips specifically, out of all body parts? In 1892, Sir Francis Galton discovered that every human fingerprint is unique. The probability of two being identical: 1 in 64 billion. They differ even in identical twins.

cards.title: Modular Narrative
cards.content: Moses' story is distributed across 30+ chapters in 10 distinct 'blocks.' Each block reads independently, yet together they form a unified whole. Modern cinema calls this 'non-linear narrative.' 1,400 years before Pulp Fiction.

cards.title: The Word Map
cards.content: The root for 'faith' (iman): 811 occurrences. The root for 'disbelief' (kufr): 525 occurrences. Ratio: 1.54 - a positive bias. Mercy: 294. Punishment: 343. The root for 'knowledge': 1.1% of all words. The Quran has a richer word distribution than Shakespeare (hapax legomena: 18-20% vs 15%).

cards.title: Time Flexibility
cards.content: In the Quran, future events are described in past tense (Prophetic Perfect) - as though they have already occurred. And past events are narrated in present tense (Historical Present) - as though happening now. The Sleepers of the Cave: 300 solar years = 309 lunar years. The difference is the exact solar-lunar calendar conversion.

cards.title: Iltifat (Shifts in Perspective)
cards.content: Sura Al-Fatiha uses 3 different perspectives in just 7 verses: third person (He), second person (You), first person plural (We). In modern literature, this is known as 'polyphonic voice.' Achieving this in 7 verses is unprecedented in literary history.

conclusion.title: Seeing the Invisible
points: Its words carry mathematical balance
Its sounds guide emotions
Its structure forms mirror symmetries
Each verse holds seven layers of meaning
It pointed to scientific truths 1,400 years in advance
Its historical details are confirmed by archaeology
Not a single unnecessary word among its 77,800
Preserved letter by letter, unchanged for 1,400 years
conclusion.question: Could this text be the work of a human being?
conclusion.verseIntro: The Quran asks this very question:

verse.arabic: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ ۚ وَلَوْ كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ لَوَجَدُوا فِيهِ اخْتِلَافًا كَثِيرًا
verse.translation: Will they not reflect upon the Quran? If it had been from anyone other than Allah, they would have found within it much contradiction.

footer.title: Quran Codex
footer.methodology: A synthesis of linguistic, mathematical, and scientific research on the Holy Quran.
sources: Abdel Razek Naufal - Numerical Miracle Research in the Quran
Raymond Farrin - Structure and Qur'anic Interpretation (2014)
Corpus Coranicum - Berlin-Brandenburg Academy of Sciences
Dr. Keith L. Moore - The Developing Human (Embryology)
Dr. Maurice Bucaille - The Bible, the Quran and Science
Quranic Arabic Corpus - University of Leeds
